duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: initializeAlphaIndices on EntropyGenerator has onlyOwner modifier but is called by TraitForgeNft._incrementGeneration, which is allowedCaller not owner, permanently blocking generation advancement after 10,000 mints
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same access control mismatch between onlyOwner on initializeAlphaIndices and the TraitForgeNft caller. TECH-H001 has the most detailed technical evidence including deployment script analysis and specific line references.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Entropy values are deterministic, stored once, and publicly readable via getPublicEntropy, allowing any user to predict and selectively mint high-value tokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same vulnerability: getPublicEntropy exposes the full entropy sequence, current indices are readable, and mints are predictable. ECON-H001 provides the most comprehensive attack economics and victim impact analysis including NukeFund extraction strategy.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: mintWithBudget uses global _tokenIds counter instead of generationMintCounts in its while-loop condition, making the function permanently non-functional after generation 1
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All identify the identical bug: while loop compares _tokenIds (global) against maxTokensPerGen (per-gen), breaking after 10,000 total mints. TECH-H003 provides the clearest technical description with exact line references and the correct fix.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Off-by-one error in listForForging uses <= instead of < when comparing forgingCounts against forgePotential, allowing every forger one extra forge beyond intended limit
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same <= vs < comparison bug in listForForging and the missing recheck in forgeWithListed. TECH-H004 provides the most precise code evidence with exact line numbers and clear explanation of the boundary condition. Note: TECH2-H004 and TECH2-H008 have duplicate IDs with other threats; the forge-off-by-one instances are the ones being grouped here.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: All contracts inherit Pausable and use whenNotPaused modifiers but none expose external pause()/unpause() functions, making the emergency stop mechanism completely non-functional
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All identify the same missing functionality: no external wrapper for internal _pause()/_unpause(). OPS-H002 provides the most comprehensive listing of all affected contracts and functions with clear operational impact analysis. Note: TECH2-H007 has a duplicate ID with another threat; the Pausable instance is grouped here.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Entropy slots are never regenerated across generations; getNextEntropy wraps currentSlotIndex to 0 after 10,010 values, causing identical entropy sequence reuse in all subsequent generations
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same index-wrapping behavior in getNextEntropy with no entropy regeneration between generations. TECH-H006 provides the most detailed technical analysis of the wrap mechanism and its relationship to _incrementGeneration. Note: TECH2-H003 has a duplicate ID; the entropy-recycling instance is grouped here.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: forgeWithListed checks msg.value >= forgingFee but does not refund excess ETH, permanently locking overpayments in EntityForging which has no withdrawal mechanism
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All identify the same missing refund in forgeWithListed and contrast it with mintToken's refund behavior. TECH-H007 provides the clearest technical comparison with mintToken's refund pattern. Note: TECH2-H006 has a duplicate ID; the excess-ETH instance is grouped here.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: EntityForging._cancelListingForForging deletes the listing struct but does not clear listedTokenIds[tokenId], leaving stale pointers that cause state confusion and potential transfer DoS via _beforeTokenTransfer interactions
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root cause: stale listedTokenIds mapping after cancellation in EntityForging. TECH-H014 adds the _beforeTokenTransfer DoS impact pathway but shares the same root cause. TECH-H008 covers both the state confusion and cross-contract hook implications most comprehensively. Note: TECH2-H008 and TECH2-H007 have duplicate IDs; their stale-pointer instances are grouped here.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: _incrementGeneration increments currentGeneration without checking against maxGeneration, allowing minting beyond the intended 10-generation cap
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All identify the same missing guard: require(currentGeneration < maxGeneration) absent from _incrementGeneration. TECH-H009 provides the most concise technical proof with exact line reference and contrast with forge()'s existing check.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: writeEntropyBatch1/2/3 are public functions with no access control, allowing anyone to call them and control the entropy sequence by choosing the block.number at write time
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing access control on batch write functions. ECON-H007 provides the most detailed attack economics and deployment window analysis, including the separate-transaction deployment pattern creating race conditions.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: NukeFund.ageMultiplier defaults to 0 and is never set in deployment, causing calculateAge to always return 0 and nullifying the hold-to-earn mechanic
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All identify the same uninitialized state variable causing age calculation to always yield 0. ECON-H003 provides the most comprehensive economic impact analysis including attack economics and the collapse of the hold-to-earn model. Note: TECH2-H005 has a duplicate ID; the ageMultiplier instance is grouped here.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: NukeFund.receive() and DevFund.receive() use require(success) on ETH transfers to devAddress/owner/daoAddress; if any recipient rejects ETH, all minting/trading/forging is permanently bricked
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same ETH transfer failure cascade through NukeFund and DevFund. TECH-H012 covers all three conditional branches in NukeFund.receive() and explicitly identifies the DevFund cascade path mentioned in ECON-H021.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TraitForgeNft._beforeTokenTransfer makes external calls to entityForgingContract that can revert if the contract is unset, misconfigured, or malfunctioning, blocking all NFT transfers including mints, burns, and trades
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same hard dependency in the ERC721 transfer hook on entityForgingContract external calls. TECH-H013 provides the most detailed analysis of the failure modes and affected lifecycle paths. Note: TECH2-H004 has a duplicate ID; the transfer-hook-DoS instance is grouped here.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: EntityTrading deletes listing structs on buy/cancel but never clears listedTokenIds mapping, creating stale pointers that can cause listing corruption and asset lockout
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same stale listedTokenIds mapping in EntityTrading after buy/cancel operations. TECH-H015 provides the most comprehensive analysis including off-chain indexer implications and relist scenarios. Note: TECH2-H005 has a duplicate ID; the EntityTrading stale-pointer instance is grouped here.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Mint price drops ~98% when a new generation starts because generationMintCounts resets to 0, creating a massive arbitrage opportunity at generation boundaries
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same price reset mechanism at generation boundaries. ECON-H014 provides more detailed attack economics including precise ROI calculations.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: NukeFund.nuke() applies the claim cap only when finalNukeFactor > nukeFactorMaxParam instead of using min(potentialClaim, maxClaim), allowing claims to exceed intended maximum when parameters are misconfigured or finalNukeFactor is high but below threshold
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same conditional cap logic defect in nuke(). OPS-H017 provides comprehensive analysis of the parameter interaction and achievable nuke factor ranges. Note: TECH2-H003 has a duplicate ID; the nuke-cap-bypass instance is grouped here.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TraitForgeNft.burn() makes unchecked external calls to airdropContract; if airdropContract is unset, misconfigured, or reverts, all burns and nuke claims are permanently blocked, locking NukeFund ETH
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same airdropContract dependency in burn() blocking nuke operations. ECON2-H020 provides more detailed economic impact analysis including NukeFund lockup implications. Note: TECH2-H006 has a duplicate ID; the airdrop-burn-DoS instance is grouped here.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: DevFund.receive() integer division truncation causes amountPerWeight=0 when msg.value < totalDevWeight, routing the entire payment to owner as remainder instead of to developers
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same precision loss in DevFund reward distribution. ECON2-H018 provides more detailed analysis including deployment configuration (totalDevWeight=300) and cumulative impact assessment.
